parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Limit system access to authorized users, processes acting on behalf of authorized users, and devices (including other systems). Ensure that only authenticated and approved entities can connect to organizational systems processing, storing, or transmitting CUI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Limit system access to the types of transactions and functions that authorized users are permitted to execute. Enforce approved authorizations for logical access to information and system resources in accordance with applicable access-control policies.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Control the flow of CUI in accordance with approved authorizations. Information flow control regulates where CUI is allowed to travel within a system and between interconnected systems, enforcing approved paths and preventing unauthorized disclosure.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Separate the duties of individuals to reduce the risk of malicious activity without collusion. Assign different people to authorize, execute, and review critical functions so that no single individual can complete a sensitive transaction alone.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Employ the principle of least privilege, including for specific security functions and privileged accounts. Grant only the minimum access rights necessary for users to perform their assigned duties and revoke access promptly when no longer required.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Use non-privileged accounts or roles when accessing non-security functions. Require administrators to use separate non-privileged accounts for routine tasks such as email, web browsing, and general office work to reduce the attack surface.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Prevent non-privileged users from executing privileged functions and capture the execution of such functions in audit logs. Ensure that elevated operations are restricted to authorized personnel and are fully traceable.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Limit unsuccessful logon attempts by enforcing account lockout policies after a defined number of consecutive invalid access attempts. Protect systems from brute-force and credential-stuffing attacks through automatic lockout or delay mechanisms.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provide privacy and security notices consistent with applicable CUI rules. Display system use notification messages or banners before granting access that describe conditions of use, monitoring policies, and potential legal consequences of unauthorized access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Use session lock with pattern-hiding displays to prevent access and viewing of data after a period of inactivity. Automatically activate a screen lock or session timeout after a configurable idle period to protect unattended workstations and terminals.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Terminate (automatically) a user session after a defined condition such as a period of inactivity, exceeding a maximum session duration, or when a user explicitly logs out. Ensure that session tokens are invalidated and resources are released.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Monitor and control remote access sessions. Employ automated mechanisms to monitor and record remote access methods, detect anomalous behavior, and alert administrators to unauthorized remote connection attempts.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Employ cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of remote access sessions. Use VPN tunnels or other encrypted channels (e.g., TLS 1.2+, IPsec) for all remote connections to systems processing CUI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Route remote access via managed access control points. Direct all remote connections through a limited number of monitored and controlled network access points to enable consistent enforcement of security policies.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Authorize remote execution of privileged commands and remote access to security-relevant information only for compelling operational needs. Document and approve all privileged remote access in advance.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Authorize wireless access prior to allowing such connections. Establish usage restrictions, configuration requirements, and connection requirements for each type of wireless access and ensure devices are approved before connecting.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Protect wireless access using authentication and encryption. Implement WPA3 or equivalent enterprise-grade wireless security protocols to safeguard CUI transmitted over wireless networks from eavesdropping and unauthorized access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Control connection of mobile devices. Establish usage restrictions, configuration requirements, connection requirements, and implementation guidance for organization-controlled mobile devices using mobile device management (MDM) solutions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Encrypt CUI on mobile devices and mobile computing platforms. Apply full-disk or container-based encryption using FIPS-validated modules to protect CUI stored on laptops, tablets, smartphones, and other portable devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Verify and control or limit connections to and use of external systems. Establish terms and conditions for authorized connections, continuously monitor external interfaces, and restrict the types of data exchanged with external systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Limit use of portable storage devices on external systems. Restrict or prohibit the use of organization-controlled portable storage devices on external systems and enforce technical controls to prevent data exfiltration via removable media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Control information posted or processed on publicly accessible systems. Ensure that CUI is not posted on publicly accessible systems, designate individuals authorized to post information, and review content before public release.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Ensure that managers, systems administrators, and users of organizational systems are made aware of the security risks associated with their activities and of the applicable policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of those systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Ensure that personnel are trained to carry out their assigned information security-related duties and responsibilities. Provide initial and ongoing role-based security training tied to each individual's system access and responsibilities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provide security awareness training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat. Educate personnel on behavioral indicators, reporting procedures, and organizational policies for handling suspected insider threats.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Create and retain system audit logs and records to the extent needed to enable the monitoring, analysis, investigation, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized system activity. Capture events such as logon attempts, privilege changes, and data access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Ensure that the actions of individual system users can be uniquely traced to those users so they can be held accountable for their actions. Associate audit records with unique user identifiers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Review and update logged events on a regular basis. Determine which events require auditing based on an ongoing assessment of risk and adjust audit configurations to capture new or emerging threat indicators.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Alert in the event of an audit logging process failure. Configure automated alerts to notify security personnel when audit mechanisms fail, disk capacity for log storage is nearing limits, or log integrity is compromised.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Correlate audit record review, analysis, and reporting processes to support organizational processes for investigation and response to suspicious activities. Use SIEM or centralized log management to identify patterns across multiple data sources.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provide audit record reduction and report generation to support on-demand analysis and reporting. Implement dashboards, automated summaries, and filtering capabilities to distill actionable insights from large volumes of audit data.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provide a system capability that compares and synchronizes internal system clocks with an authoritative source to generate time stamps for audit records. Use NTP or PTP with trusted time servers to ensure consistent and accurate timestamps.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Protect audit information and audit logging tools from unauthorized access, modification, and deletion. Apply access controls, integrity checks, and encryption to audit logs so they remain tamper-evident and forensically reliable.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Limit management of audit logging functionality to a subset of privileged users. Restrict the ability to configure, disable, or modify audit mechanisms to authorized security administrators only and log all changes to audit configurations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational systems (including hardware, software, firmware, and documentation) throughout the respective system development life cycles. Track deviations from approved baselines.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish and enforce security configuration settings for information technology products employed in organizational systems. Apply hardening benchmarks (e.g., CIS, DISA STIGs) and verify configurations through automated scanning.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Track, review, approve or disapprove, and log changes to organizational systems. Implement a formal change management process that documents requests, impact assessments, approvals, and implementation verification for all system modifications.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Analyze the security impact of changes prior to implementation. Evaluate proposed changes to determine potential effects on the security posture of the system, including new vulnerabilities or degraded controls, before authorizing deployment.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Define, document, approve, and enforce physical and logical access restrictions associated with changes to organizational systems. Limit who can implement changes and ensure that change actions are logged and reversible.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Employ the principle of least functionality by configuring organizational systems to provide only essential capabilities. Disable or remove unnecessary ports, protocols, services, and software components to reduce the attack surface.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Restrict, disable, or prevent the use of nonessential programs, functions, ports, protocols, and services. Maintain an approved software and service list and block execution of unauthorized applications and network services.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Apply deny-by-exception (blacklisting) or allow-by-exception (whitelisting) policies to prevent the use of unauthorized software. Implement application control technologies to enforce organizational software execution policies.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Control and monitor user-installed software. Establish policies governing what software users may install, enforce technical restrictions through endpoint management tools, and audit installed applications for compliance.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Identify system users, processes acting on behalf of users, and devices. Assign unique identifiers to all entities that access organizational systems to support accountability and audit traceability.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Authenticate (or verify) the identities of users, processes, or devices as a prerequisite to allowing access to organizational systems. Validate credentials using approved authentication mechanisms before granting system access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Use multifactor authentication for local and network access to privileged accounts and for network access to non-privileged accounts. Combine two or more factors (something you know, have, or are) to strengthen identity verification.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Employ replay-resistant authentication mechanisms for network access to privileged and non-privileged accounts. Use protocols such as Kerberos, TLS with mutual authentication, or challenge-response mechanisms that prevent credential replay.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Prevent reuse of identifiers for a defined period. Ensure that previously assigned user IDs, device identifiers, and account names are not reassigned to new entities until a sufficient time has elapsed to avoid ambiguity in audit records.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Disable identifiers after a defined period of inactivity. Automatically disable or remove user accounts and device identifiers that have been inactive for an organization-defined time period to reduce the risk of unauthorized access.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Enforce a minimum password complexity and change of characters when new passwords are created. Require passwords to meet length, complexity, and composition rules that align with current NIST guidelines (e.g., minimum 12 characters).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Prohibit password reuse for a specified number of generations. Maintain a password history and prevent users from reusing any of their previous passwords within the organization-defined number of password changes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Allow temporary password use for system logons with an immediate change to a permanent password. Issue one-time or time-limited passwords for initial access or password resets and require users to set a new password upon first use.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Store and transmit only cryptographically-protected passwords. Hash passwords using approved algorithms (e.g., bcrypt, scrypt, PBKDF2) with unique salts and transmit credentials only over encrypted channels.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Obscure feedback of authentication information during the authentication process. Mask password entry fields and avoid displaying authentication credentials in plaintext on screens, in logs, or in error messages.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish an operational incident-handling capability for organizational systems that includes preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response activities. Maintain an incident response plan that is regularly tested.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Track, document, and report incidents to designated officials and/or authorities both internal and external to the organization. Define reporting timelines, escalation paths, and communication templates for different incident severity levels.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Test the organizational incident response capability. Conduct tabletop exercises, simulations, or full-scale tests at least annually to validate the effectiveness of the incident response plan and identify areas for improvement.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Perform maintenance on organizational systems. Schedule and conduct regular preventive and corrective maintenance on hardware, software, and firmware in accordance with manufacturer recommendations and organizational policies.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provide controls on the tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel used to conduct system maintenance. Approve, monitor, and log all maintenance activities and ensure maintenance tools are inspected and authorized before use.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Ensure equipment removed for off-site maintenance is sanitized of any CUI. Purge or destroy data on storage media before releasing hardware for external repair, following NIST SP 800-88 sanitization guidelines.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Check media containing diagnostic and test programs for malicious code before the media are used in organizational systems. Scan maintenance media with up-to-date anti-malware tools before connecting to production environments.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Require multifactor authentication to establish nonlocal maintenance sessions via external network connections and terminate such connections when nonlocal maintenance is complete. Log all remote maintenance sessions.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Supervise the maintenance activities of maintenance personnel without required access authorization. Escort and monitor non-cleared maintenance workers and verify that their work does not introduce vulnerabilities or compromise CUI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Protect (i.e., physically control and securely store) system media containing CUI, both paper and digital. Store media in locked containers, secure areas, or encrypted volumes and restrict access to authorized personnel.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Limit access to CUI on system media to authorized users. Implement access controls on digital media and physical safeguards on paper media to prevent unauthorized individuals from viewing or copying CUI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Sanitize or destroy system media containing CUI before disposal or release for reuse. Apply sanitization techniques (clear, purge, or destroy) per NIST SP 800-88 based on the media type and sensitivity level.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Mark media with necessary CUI markings and distribution limitations. Apply appropriate CUI designation indicators, handling caveats, and dissemination controls on both physical and digital media.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Control access to media containing CUI and maintain accountability for media during transport outside of controlled areas. Track media using logs, chain-of-custody forms, or asset management systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of CUI stored on digital media during transport unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards. Use FIPS 140-validated encryption for portable storage devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Control the use of removable media on system components. Establish policies restricting removable media types, enforce technical controls to block unauthorized devices, and scan all approved removable media before use.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Prohibit the use of portable storage devices when such devices have no identifiable owner. Ensure all removable media can be traced to an accountable individual and block anonymous or unregistered devices.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Protect the confidentiality of backup CUI at storage locations. Encrypt backups using FIPS-validated cryptographic modules, restrict access to backup media, and store copies in physically secure off-site locations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access to organizational systems containing CUI. Conduct background checks, verify employment eligibility, and assess trustworthiness before granting system access to new personnel.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Ensure that organizational systems containing CUI are protected during and after personnel actions such as terminations and transfers. Revoke access promptly, retrieve credentials and equipment, and conduct exit interviews.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Limit physical access to organizational systems, equipment, and the respective operating environments to authorized individuals. Use physical barriers, badge readers, biometrics, and security guards to enforce facility access controls.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Protect and monitor the physical facility and support infrastructure for organizational systems. Deploy surveillance cameras, intrusion detection sensors, and environmental monitoring to safeguard data-processing areas.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Escort visitors and monitor visitor activity. Require visitors to sign in, wear identification badges, and be accompanied by authorized personnel when in areas containing systems that process, store, or transmit CUI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Maintain audit logs of physical access. Record entry and exit events at facility access points using electronic badge logs or sign-in sheets and retain records for the organization-defined retention period.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Control and manage physical access devices such as keys, locks, combinations, and card readers. Inventory all physical access devices, change combinations and keys periodically, and deactivate lost or compromised credentials immediately.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Enforce safeguarding measures for CUI at alternate work sites. Apply physical and technical security controls at telework locations, home offices, and satellite facilities equivalent to those at the primary work site.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Periodically assess the risk to organizational operations (including mission, functions, image, or reputation), organizational assets, and individuals resulting from the operation of organizational systems and the processing, storage, or transmission of CUI.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Scan for vulnerabilities in organizational systems and applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities affecting those systems and applications are identified. Use automated scanning tools and maintain current vulnerability databases.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Remediate vulnerabilities in accordance with risk assessments. Prioritize and track remediation of identified vulnerabilities based on severity, exploitability, and potential impact to CUI, and verify that fixes are applied within defined timeframes.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Periodically assess the security controls in organizational systems to determine if the controls are effective in their application. Conduct assessments using agreed-upon procedures and document findings for remediation.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Develop and implement plans of action designed to correct deficiencies and reduce or eliminate vulnerabilities in organizational systems. Track POA&M items to closure with defined milestones and responsible parties.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Monitor security controls on an ongoing basis to ensure the continued effectiveness of the controls. Implement continuous monitoring strategies that include automated tools, periodic assessments, and status reporting.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Develop, document, and periodically update system security plans that describe system boundaries, system environments of operation, how security requirements are implemented, and the relationships with or connections to other systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Monitor, control, and protect communications at the external boundaries and key internal boundaries of organizational systems. Deploy firewalls, intrusion detection and prevention systems, and proxies at network perimeters.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Employ architectural designs, software development techniques, and systems engineering principles that promote effective information security within organizational systems. Segment networks to isolate CUI processing environments.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Separate user functionality from system management functionality. Isolate administrative interfaces and management planes from end-user interfaces to prevent unauthorized access to system internals.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources. Implement controls to clear shared memory, temporary storage, and cache between user sessions to prevent information leakage.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Deny network communications traffic by default and allow network communications traffic by exception (i.e., deny all, permit by exception). Configure firewalls and access control lists to block all traffic not explicitly authorized.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Deny network communications traffic by default and allow network communications traffic by exception at managed interfaces. Apply a default-deny posture on all network boundaries and document each permitted traffic flow.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Prevent remote devices from simultaneously establishing non-remote connections with organizational systems and communicating via some other connection to resources in external networks (i.e., split tunneling).

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI during transmission unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards. Use TLS 1.2+, IPsec, or SSH for all data in transit.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Terminate network connections associated with communications sessions at the end of the sessions or after a defined period of inactivity. Configure idle timeouts on network devices, VPN gateways, and application servers.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Establish and manage cryptographic keys for cryptography employed in organizational systems. Implement key generation, distribution, storage, rotation, revocation, and destruction procedures using hardware security modules where appropriate.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Employ FIPS-validated cryptography when used to protect the confidentiality of CUI. Use cryptographic modules validated under FIPS 140-2 or FIPS 140-3 for encryption, hashing, and digital signature operations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Prohibit remote activation of collaborative computing devices and provide indication of devices in use to users present at the device. Disable microphones and cameras by default and provide physical or visual indicators when active.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Control and monitor the use of mobile code. Define acceptable and unacceptable mobile code technologies, establish usage restrictions, and implement controls to prevent the execution of unauthorized mobile code.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Control and monitor the use of Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) technologies. Establish usage restrictions, encrypt VoIP traffic, segment VoIP networks, and monitor for unauthorized use or eavesdropping.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Protect the authenticity of communications sessions. Implement mechanisms such as digital signatures, message authentication codes, or TLS to verify the identity of communication endpoints and detect message tampering.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Protect the confidentiality of CUI at rest. Encrypt stored data using FIPS-validated cryptographic mechanisms such as AES-256, implement access controls on storage systems, and manage encryption keys securely.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Identify, report, and correct system flaws in a timely manner. Establish a patch management process that identifies applicable patches, tests them in a staging environment, and deploys them within organization-defined timeframes based on severity.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Provide protection from malicious code at designated locations within organizational systems. Deploy anti-malware solutions on endpoints, servers, and network boundaries with real-time scanning and automated quarantine capabilities.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Monitor system security alerts and advisories and take action in response. Subscribe to vulnerability feeds (e.g., CISA, NVD, vendor advisories), assess applicability, and implement recommended mitigations promptly.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available. Configure anti-malware tools for automatic signature and engine updates and verify that definitions are current across all protected systems.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Perform periodic scans of organizational systems and real-time scans of files from external sources as files are downloaded, opened, or executed. Schedule full system scans and enable on-access scanning for file operations.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Monitor organizational systems, including inbound and outbound communications traffic, to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks. Deploy network monitoring, IDS/IPS, and traffic analysis tools at key network boundaries.

parts.name: statement
parts.prose: Identify unauthorized use of organizational systems. Implement monitoring mechanisms that detect anomalous account activity, unauthorized software execution, and policy violations, and alert security personnel for investigation.
